'''Amir Bourouh''' (born 5 January 2001) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously spent time on loan from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Bourouh was born in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire, England]], but considers neighbouring [[Halifax, West Yorkshire|Halifax]] as his hometown.

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
In 2019 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921735|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 26-30 Wigan Warriors|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

===Halifax===
On 28 January 2021, it was reported that he had joined [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship]] for a season-long loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Jan 2021|accessdate=28 Jan 2021|title=Wigan youngster Amir Bourouh joins hometown club Halifax on loan|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-youngster-amir-bourouh-joins-hometown-club-halifax-on-loan/}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 19 August 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Aug 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Salford sign Amir Bourouh from Wigan for 2022|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-sign-amir-bourouh-from-wigan-for-2022/}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders (loan)===
On 21 June 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the RFL Championship on short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Jun 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Super League loan watch: Salford Red Devils hooker makes short-term Championship move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-players-who-have-agreed-loan-dual-registration-deals}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
On 24 June 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in the Super League on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=24 Jun 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Hull FC sign hooker Bourouh from Salford for 2025|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cneeerykyy8o}}</ref>
